prompt_patterns: draw a series circuit with a battery switch and light bulb; simple circuit battery switch bulb; switch from negative terminal
diagram_goal: One complete loop with battery, switch, and bulb in series. Put switch on the negative side if requested.
svg_hint: single rectangular loop; battery left; switch bottom or negative side; bulb top or right
tutor_questions: Is it series or parallel?; Where is the battery?; Where will the switch open or close the one path?; Is the bulb in the same loop?
checklist: one path; battery included; switch included; bulb included; closed path unless open switch requested

prompt_patterns: draw a series circuit with battery switch resistor; simple circuit battery switch resistor
diagram_goal: One complete loop with battery, switch, and resistor in series.
svg_hint: single rectangular loop; resistor as zigzag/rectangle on top or right; switch on negative side if requested
tutor_questions: What is the load: resistor or bulb?; Is every part on one path?; Is the switch able to open the whole loop?
checklist: one path; battery; switch; resistor; complete loop

prompt_patterns: draw a series circuit with battery switch motor; simple circuit with motor
diagram_goal: One complete loop with battery, switch, and motor in series.
svg_hint: motor symbol circle M; battery left; switch bottom
tutor_questions: Is the motor in the only path?; Can the switch turn the whole circuit off?

prompt_patterns: draw series circuit with ammeter; ammeter in a simple circuit; measure current with ammeter
diagram_goal: Ammeter placed in series inside the same loop as the bulb.
svg_hint: circle A in the main loop; not across the bulb
tutor_questions: What are you measuring: current or voltage?; Where must current flow through the ammeter?; Is the ammeter in series?
checklist: ammeter in series; one path; bulb in loop; battery in loop
source_ref: PDF p.14, p.17, p.22; worksheet photos

prompt_patterns: add a voltmeter to test voltage of the motor; measure voltage of motor; voltmeter across motor
diagram_goal: Main circuit remains series; voltmeter connects across both sides of the motor.
ascii_template: main loop: +| |−---/---(M)---back; voltmeter: (V) connected across M
svg_hint: draw motor in loop; draw voltmeter in a small parallel connection across motor terminals
tutor_questions: What part are you measuring?; Where are the two sides of the motor?; Can the voltmeter touch both sides of the motor?
checklist: voltmeter across motor; voltmeter parallel to measured part; switch controls loop

prompt_patterns: add a voltmeter to test voltage of the battery; measure voltage of battery; voltmeter across battery
diagram_goal: Voltmeter connects across the battery terminals or across the part named in the prompt.
ascii_template: main loop: +| |−---/---(bulb)---back; voltmeter: (V) across battery terminals
svg_hint: voltmeter forms a small parallel path across battery or bulb, not the only series path
tutor_questions: Are you measuring the battery or the bulb?; Where are the two terminals?; Is the voltmeter across those terminals?
checklist: voltmeter across target; main loop complete; switch controls loop

prompt_patterns: draw a parallel circuit with battery switch two bulbs; parallel circuit with two light bulbs; two bulbs on separate branches
diagram_goal: Two separate branches, each with one bulb, connected across the battery. Main switch can turn off whole circuit if requested.
ascii_template: battery and main switch on side; top/bottom rails; branch1 has bulb; branch2 has bulb
svg_hint: two vertical branches between top and bottom rails; bulb in each branch; battery at left; optional main switch before branches
tutor_questions: How many branches do you need?; Should each bulb have its own path?; Where can a switch turn off the whole circuit?
checklist: two branches; bulbs on separate branches; battery connected to rails; main switch before/after branch section if whole-circuit switch requested

components: battery; three light bulbs; three switches
prompt_patterns: parallel circuit with three light bulbs and three switches; each light can be turned off individually; 3 bulbs with individual switches
diagram_goal: Three parallel branches, each branch has a light bulb and its own switch.
ascii_template: battery left; rails; three branches; each branch has bulb + switch
svg_hint: branch columns; each branch contains bulb then switch; all branches connect to common rails
tutor_questions: How many lights?; Does each light need its own branch?; Does each branch need its own switch?
checklist: three branches; one bulb per branch; one switch per branch; battery connected to rails

components: two batteries; three light bulbs; one resistor; three switches
prompt_patterns: parallel circuit with two batteries 3 bulbs 1 resistor 3 switches; each light individually
diagram_goal: Parallel circuit with three bulb branches and a resistor included as requested; switches control individual branches if requested.
ascii_template: battery stack left; rails; branches for bulbs and resistor; switches in branch paths
svg_hint: use rails with multiple vertical branches; label resistor and each bulb/switch
tutor_questions: Which parts need their own branch?; Are the bulbs separate?; What should each switch control?
checklist: parallel branches; two batteries shown; resistor included; switches match prompt

prompt_patterns: two bulbs in parallel and a third bulb in series; complex circuit with two parallel bulbs and one series bulb
diagram_goal: One bulb is in series with a parallel section containing two bulbs.
ascii_template: battery---switch---bulb3---split to bulb1 and bulb2 branches---join---back
svg_hint: draw series bulb before branch split; two parallel branches after it; then branches rejoin
tutor_questions: Which bulb must be on every path?; Which two bulbs need separate branches?; Where do the branches split and rejoin?
checklist: one bulb in series before/after branch; two bulbs in parallel; battery; switch controls whole circuit

components: two batteries; three light bulbs; ammeter; resistor; switch
prompt_patterns: series circuit with 3 bulbs ammeter resistor switch two batteries; draw a series circuit with 3 light bulbs an ammeter a resistor a switch and two batteries
diagram_goal: All components in one path. Ammeter is in series anywhere in the loop.
svg_hint: single rectangular loop with all components placed along it
tutor_questions: List the components.; Since it is series, how many paths?; Where can the ammeter go so all current passes through it?
checklist: one path; two batteries; three bulbs; ammeter in series; resistor; switch

components: wire; battery; switch; bulb; resistor; ammeter; voltmeter; motor
prompt_patterns: what symbols should I use; circuit diagram symbols; symbol for resistor
diagram_goal: Return symbol reference and a short reminder of what each symbol means.
ascii_template: wire: line; battery: long/short lines; switch: break; bulb: circle X; resistor: zigzag; ammeter: circle A; voltmeter: circle V; motor: circle M
tutor_questions: Which component are you trying to draw?; Is it a meter, source, load, or connector?
checklist: use standard symbol; label if needed; keep connections clear

prompt_patterns: label current flow; which way does current flow; show current direction
diagram_goal: Add arrows around the closed path. Use conventional current from positive terminal to negative terminal unless class notes define electron flow.
ascii_template: arrows leave + terminal, travel through circuit, return to − terminal
svg_hint: place arrowheads on wires around the loop/branches
tutor_questions: Are we labeling conventional current or electron flow?; Where is the positive battery terminal?; Is the circuit closed?
checklist: arrows on complete path; start at positive terminal for conventional current; branch arrows in parallel

prompt_patterns: help me draw a circuit; tutor me through drawing; ask me questions to draw circuit
diagram_goal: Guided circuit drawing mode that asks one question at a time and builds the diagram plan.
svg_hint: collect circuit type, components, meters, switches, and labels first
tutor_questions: Is the circuit series, parallel, or complex?; What components are inside it?; Which switch controls the whole circuit or one branch?; Are you measuring current or voltage?; Should current be labeled?
checklist: identify circuit type; list components; place battery; place switches; place meters correctly; check path count
source_ref: teacher request; PDF p.13-p.17; worksheet photos
